capacity_evidence.device: one Blackhole p300c in a 1x1 TTNN mesh
capacity_evidence.failure_kind: TT_FATAL DRAM allocator out of memory
capacity_evidence.explanation: Serialized full translated-layer probes at the preserved batch of 32 passed through device-to-host output materialization at sequence 6528 and failed at the next tile-aligned length, 6560, when the MLP multiply requested a 9.01 GiB bf16 output buffer. At the HF limit, one [batch,32768,23040] bf16 MLP buffer is 45 GiB and gate, up, and product buffers alone require 135 GiB before residuals, weights, caches, and allocator overhead.

optimized_capacity_evidence.device: one Blackhole p300c in a 1x1 TTNN mesh
optimized_capacity_evidence.selected_weight_policy: BFP4_B LoFi projections; prefill weights DRAM interleaved and decode weights DRAM width-sharded
optimized_capacity_evidence.validation: full optimized dense layer with persistent key/value caches and a device-resident output of shape [1,32,6528,3072]
optimized_capacity_evidence.large_m_strategy: attention matmuls use TTNN-selected bounded large-M configs above 1024 rows; dense MLP rows are processed in chunks of at most 1024 and concatenated on device
optimized_capacity_evidence.explanation: The optimized dtype/layout policy changes persistent-cache and weight capacity, so the advertised functional boundary was rerun and passes. The first optimized attempt exposed an explicit large-M L1 circular-buffer request; the bounded large-M implementation fixed it. The optimized pass did not claim a larger context because the complete optimized boundary above 6528 was not re-characterized. Advertised capability is unchanged.

multichip_capacity_evidence.target_mesh: four Blackhole p300c devices in a fixed 1x4 FABRIC_1D_RING mesh
multichip_capacity_evidence.selected_weight_policy: BFP4_B LoFi projections; rank-local column/row tensor parallelism
multichip_capacity_evidence.kv_cache_dtype_layout: BFP8_B TILE DRAM, one of four KV heads per device

stack_residual_contract.distribution: replicated values across TP ranks; width-sharded within each device
stack_residual_contract.ownership: decode_forward_to_residual produces this layout; decode_forward_from_residual consumes it directly; only the stack exit calls materialize_decode_output

persistent_async_all_reduce_pool.dtype_layout: BF16 TILE L1 width-sharded on the same 32 residual cores
persistent_async_all_reduce_pool.sub_device_manager: one manager per mesh pool covering all 110 worker cores; subdevice 0 is the active stall group
persistent_async_all_reduce_pool.sharing: one DecodeAllReduceResources pool is shared by every sequential decoder layer; the first layer is the owner and later layers are non-owning users
persistent_async_all_reduce_pool.lifetime: created lazily before first decode/trace capture and retained for trace replay and the decoder-stack lifetime
persistent_async_all_reduce_pool.cleanup: release non-owning layers first, then the owner deallocates the intermediate, resets the stall group, and clears the loaded sub-device manager
persistent_async_all_reduce_pool.context_effect: L1-only allocation; it does not consume DRAM-backed paged-KV capacity or reduce the 32768-token context

optimized_multichip_runtime_resources.validation: two independently materialized decoder instances share one pool and preserve the residual contract at PCC 1.0 with zero inter-layer collectives

conservative_reserved_headroom_budget.assumptions: Both 131072x3072 untied vocabulary matrices are conservatively replicated in BF16 even though the later full-model stage may shard or quantize them. RoPE is shared across all layers through the decoder's rope_cache argument. Decode activation/CCL reserve is a reusable per-layer peak allowance, not multiplied by 40; the 512 MiB reservation is far above the sub-megabyte logical batch-32 residual and local MLP tensors. The independent 4 GiB allocator/program/fragmentation margin is retained after the configured 100 MB trace region.

executed_vs_projected_boundary.executed_full_context: batch 1, full 32768-token prefill, paged K/V sampling, and decode at position 32767
executed_vs_projected_boundary.executed_batch32: logical prefill length 31 plus paged decode positions 31 and 32 with warm trace replay
executed_vs_projected_boundary.projected_not_executed_in_this_stage: 40-layer batch-32 32768-token persistent K/V allocation and full-model decode
executed_vs_projected_boundary.scope_reason: Allocating and scheduling the 40-layer stack is the later full-model goal. This decoder stage proves the rank-local cache shape/bytes and uses the measured TTNN allocator capacity plus conservative non-decoder reservations to establish fit without claiming a full-model execution. Full-context batch-32 prefill activation scheduling is also not claimed.

decoder_stage_batch32_full_stack_memory_.explanation: Historical decoder-stage projection retained for provenance only and superseded by full_model_capacity_evidence below. The full-model stage measured the complete terminal, all decoder weights, generator staging allocations, and the batch-32 full-context KV cache against the current allocator.

multichip_capacity_evidence.explanation: The target mesh executes the full HF-advertised logical context with paged local-head cache, nontrivial page-table mapping, sampled HF K/V correctness through the final page, and decode at the final valid position. No aligned-only public contract is introduced. The supported context is therefore extended to the HF model limit rather than carrying forward the single-chip DRAM boundary.

full_model_capacity_evidence.target_mesh: four Blackhole p300c devices in a fixed 1x4 FABRIC_1D_RING mesh

batch32_hf_context_allocation.cache_dtype_layout: BFP8_B TILE DRAM, one local KV head per rank
batch32_hf_context_allocation.scope: Physical allocation proof for simultaneous batch-32 persistent capacity. Full 32K prefill execution is advertised and exercised at batch 1; mixed active-32 execution is exercised separately at logical lengths 33 through 64.

full_model_capacity_evidence.decision: No reduction. The largest feasible context equals the HF architectural limit of 32768 tokens. The complete batch-1 path executes the non-aligned 32767-token boundary plus decode, and all 32 users' full persistent weight/KV allocation fits with 4.122 GB per-device DRAM headroom.

notes: The 6,528-token result remains the historical measured batch-32 single-device boundary, not a Falcon3 architecture limit. The fixed TP4 full model preserves the optimized decoder plan and supports the HF limit of 32,768. The public generator physically executes a non-aligned 32,767-token prompt through all 40 layers, owns padding/chunking/cache fill/positions/output slicing, and decodes at the boundary. Batch-32 full-context weights plus KV cache are physically allocated on every rank, and active-32 mixed non-aligned prompts are executed with exact slot/page/reset controls. No context reduction is necessary.
